principle: A term is LEFT UNTRANSLATED (transliterated + openable DPD sense-gloss) iff it is (a) technical, (b) genuinely non-1:1 with English, AND (c) an established loanword in the scholarly/practitioner creole. Such terms are handled by policy, not by a per-token translation decision. This is one avowed, auditable, forkable commitment — the sibling of the aliases table.

out.gaṇa: has a clean English equivalent (company/following); not a loanword
out.pāsāda: a REGISTER choice (palace/longhouse), not a fixed technical concept
out.vedehiputta: an interpretive epithet/name, needs a rendering decision

debatable.paccaya: technical Paṭṭhāna relation-name, but also cleanly 'condition' — boundary case; the membership decision is itself the meta-choice-point
debatable.rūpa: leave-in as an aggregate, but 'form/matter/visible' elsewhere — context-dependent
debatable.sukha: often translated 'pleasant/ease'; leave-in mainly in feeling-triad
debatable.sutta: leave-in; but 'discourse' is widely accepted too

note: score_v4 uses core + debatable as the active leave-in set; the debatable list is flagged so a forked edition can move the boundary and re-score.
